title: Assessment Items - Child Abduction / Missing Infant Response (Code Pink)

items.question: What is the first required action when a report of a missing or abducted child or infant is received?
choices: Wait for a second report from another department before acting.
Identify the exact incident location and begin immediate response.
Delay action until external authorities arrive.
Start documenting the report without initiating any search.
items.rationale: The first step is to determine the exact location and begin immediate response.

items.question: How should the initial internal search be conducted?
choices: Search only the room where the child was last seen.
Direct nursing staff and security personnel to search carefully throughout the building while observing suspicious behavior.
Wait until CCTV footage is reviewed before starting any search.
Search only public waiting areas.
items.rationale: The internal search must be careful, wide, and coordinated with observation of suspicious behavior.

items.question: What access-control measure must be applied immediately?
choices: Close all exits, main entrances, emergency exits, and elevators except for authorized access.
Close only the main gate and leave elevators operating normally.
Allow unrestricted movement until the child is confirmed missing.
Shut down CCTV instead of controlling exits.
items.rationale: The procedure requires immediate control of exits, entrances, emergency exits, and elevators.

items.question: What is the role of the CCTV team during Code Pink?
choices: Archive previous footage only after the incident is closed.
Enhance surveillance and track movement in all relevant internal areas based on available information.
Monitor only the nursery area.
Use CCTV only if the child is not found after 24 hours.
items.rationale: The CCTV team must intensify surveillance immediately and track movement based on incident information.

items.question: How should Code Pink be announced inside the facility?
choices: By sending a private email to department heads only.
Through the internal overhead speaker system with repeated announcements.
Through verbal communication to reception only.
No announcement is needed until police arrive.
items.rationale: The code must be announced through the overhead speaker system and repeated to alert all staff.

items.question: When must specialized security authorities be contacted?
choices: Only if the child is not found after the first hour.
Immediately through the approved emergency numbers.
After all internal documentation is complete.
Only after facility leadership gives written approval.
items.rationale: The specialized security authorities must be contacted without delay.

items.question: Why may female security staff be specifically requested during the search process?
choices: To replace all other security staff in the facility.
To inspect female companions or visitors when required as part of the search process.
To prepare the final quality report.
To manage CCTV equipment.
items.rationale: Female security staff may be needed for proper inspection of female companions or visitors.

items.question: What should security do if the child is found with a suspicious person?
choices: Allow the person to leave if they claim to be a relative.
Stop the person, verify identity, and keep them under control until specialized authorities arrive.
Take a photo and continue searching elsewhere.
Release the child and ignore the adult.
items.rationale: The suspicious person must be stopped, identified, and controlled until authorities arrive.

items.question: What is required if the child is not found during the initial internal search?
choices: Close the case and reopen normal operations.
Expand the search outside the facility and provide the authorities with all incident details and actions already taken.
Stop all communication until new evidence appears.
Limit the search to parking areas only.
items.rationale: If the child is not found internally, the search must expand externally and authorities must receive full details.

items.question: What coordination measure should facility leadership take during the incident?
choices: Nominate a security coordinator to communicate directly with specialized security authorities.
Transfer all communication to visitors.
Stop coordination until police complete the investigation.
Assign the role to any available volunteer.
items.rationale: A member or coordinator from the security team should be nominated to coordinate with specialized authorities.

items.question: What information-support action is required during the investigation?
choices: Coordinate with specialists to access person-related information and review CCTV recordings and supporting records.
Delete CCTV footage after review to protect privacy.
Restrict all information to one department only.
Avoid using supporting records until after the case is closed.
items.rationale: The investigation requires coordinated access to relevant information, CCTV, and supporting records.

items.question: Why must security presence remain intensified inside and outside the facility during the incident?
choices: Because media coverage requires visible staffing.
Because the investigation may take time and the offender may return to the scene.
Because all staff must be reassigned to security duties.
Because visitor services must remain suspended for a week.
items.rationale: Security presence remains intensified due to the possibility of return and the extended duration of the investigation.

items.question: Which departments must contribute to the joint incident record?
choices: Security, Patient Experience, Nursing, and Quality.
Only Security and Maintenance.
Only Nursing and Reception.
Only the emergency department.
items.rationale: The joint incident record includes Security, Patient Experience, Nursing, and Quality.

items.question: What should be obtained after the specialized authorities complete the investigation and close the case?
choices: A copy of the final report including observations and recommendations.
Only a verbal summary from the shift supervisor.
A patient discharge report.
No records are needed once the case is closed.
items.rationale: A final report with observations and recommendations should be obtained after investigation closure.

items.question: What is the correct post-incident improvement action?
choices: Archive the event without analysis.
Study and analyze the incident and implement recommendations to reduce recurrence.
Avoid discussing the case internally.
Reopen all exits immediately without review.
items.rationale: The facility should analyze the incident and implement recommendations to reduce the chance of recurrence.

items.question: Which organization must receive the required final incident information?
choices: National Center for Emergency and Health Crisis Management.
Parking Department.
Food Services Department.
Front Desk Reception.
items.rationale: The final step is to provide the National Center with the required incident information.
